solution:
```java
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;

class Solution {
    public List<Integer> splitIntoFibonacci(String num) {
        int len = num.length();
        for (int i = 1; i <= len - 2; i++) {
            String aStr = num.substring(0, i);
            if (aStr.length() > 1 && aStr.charAt(0) == '0') {
                continue;
            }
            long aLong;
            try {
                aLong = Long.parseLong(aStr);
            } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
                continue;
            }
            if (aLong > Integer.MAX_VALUE || aLong < Integer.MIN_VALUE) {
                continue;
            }
            int a = (int) aLong;

            for (int j = 1; j <= len - i - 1; j++) {
                int bEnd = i + j;
                if (bEnd > len) {
                    break;
                }
                String bStr = num.substring(i, bEnd);
                if (bStr.length() > 1 && bStr.charAt(0) == '0') {
                    continue;
                }
                long bLong;
                try {
                    bLong = Long.parseLong(bStr);
                } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
                    continue;
                }
                if (bLong > Integer.MAX_VALUE || bLong < Integer.MIN_VALUE) {
                    continue;
                }
                int b = (int) bLong;

                List<Integer> result = new ArrayList<>();
                result.add(a);
                result.add(b);
                int pos = bEnd;
                int prev1 = a;
                int prev2 = b;
                boolean valid = true;

                while (pos < len) {
                    long sum = (long) prev1 + (long) prev2;
                    if (sum > Integer.MAX_VALUE) {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                    int sumInt = (int) sum;
                    String sumStr = Integer.toString(sumInt);
                    if (num.startsWith(sumStr, pos)) {
                        result.add(sumInt);
                        prev1 = prev2;
                        prev2 = sumInt;
                        pos += sumStr.length();
                    } else {
                        valid = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }

                if (valid && pos == len && result.size() >= 3) {
                    return result;
                }
            }
        }
        return new ArrayList<>();
    }
}
```

efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n^3)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: The solution iterates over possible splits for the first two numbers, then checks if the remaining string forms a valid Fibonacci-like sequence. It efficiently handles leading zeros and integer overflow, ensuring correctness while maintaining manageable performance for the input size constraints.
